market_context: The Nifty 200 is currently experiencing volatility driven by persistent FII selling and global geopolitical tensions, particularly in West Asia, which has also kept crude oil prices firm. Despite these headwinds, domestic institutional investors are aggressively buying, providing a crucial counterbalance and cushioning foreign outflows, supported by India's robust Q2 FY26 GDP growth driven by domestic demand and government spending. Falling knife investors should closely monitor developments in the US-Iran conflict and global energy prices this week, as these factors are highly sensitive to market sentiment and could dictate short-term market direction.

stocks.name: Jio Financial Services Limited
stocks.why_fell: Jio Financial Services' shares have been declining due to high valuations and weak Q2 FY26 results where net profit saw only a modest rise despite a revenue jump. The stock also experienced a significant fall in Q3 FY26 due to the impact of dividend income and share of associates and joint ventures.
stocks.value_trap_risk: High — primarily due to extremely high valuations (P/E > 90), 'N/A' ROE and OCF, and the fall being company-specific rather than purely macro-driven.
stocks.thesis_break: Net profit growth turning negative in the next two quarters or a significant increase in non-performing assets.

stocks.name: LTIMindtree Ltd
stocks.why_fell: LTIMindtree shares fell sharply in January 2026 due to a one-time hit from the implementation of new labour codes, which significantly impacted reported net profit for Q3 FY26. Additionally, brokerages flagged concerns around margins and near-term earnings visibility, while broader Indian IT sector weakness from geopolitical tensions and crude prices also contributed.
stocks.value_trap_risk: Low — the fall is primarily attributed to a one-time accounting charge and broader sector sentiment, with strong underlying operational performance and a healthy balance sheet.
stocks.thesis_break: Operating margins showing continuous decline below 15% in Q4 FY26 results or a sustained freeze in client spending.

stocks.name: Nuvama Wealth Management Limited
stocks.why_fell: Nuvama's share price fell due to valuation concerns, flat Q3 FY26 results, and significant promoter share pledging. A stock split in December 2025 also mechanically reduced the share price, further contributing to the decline.
stocks.value_trap_risk: High — due to extremely high promoter pledging (62.8%), 'N/A' ROE (reported 13.7% in external search), and an exceptionally high debt-to-equity ratio (237.583) from provided data.
stocks.thesis_break: Promoter pledge increasing further, significant client attrition, or substantial regulatory action against wealth management practices.
